description_long: This house features a simple rectangular brick structure with a prominent red hip roof and a single large brick chimney. The walls are constructed from reddish-brown brick, and the roof is supported by visible wooden beams and posts, giving the house a sturdy, rustic appearance. The roof extends well beyond the walls, providing ample overhang and shade around the perimeter. There are large open sections in the walls, suggesting unfinished construction or a pavilion-like design, with no visible windows or doors. The chimney is positioned off-center, adding a touch of asymmetry to the otherwise balanced form. The overall design is utilitarian, with minimal decorative elements and a focus on structural clarity.
description: A rectangular brick house with a red hip roof, wooden supports, and a large off-center chimney. The structure is open-sided with no visible windows or doors.
description_view_0: The house is seen from the gable end, showing the red hip roof, brick wall, and the chimney emerging from the right side.
description_view_1: The front view displays the full width of the house, the central chimney, and a large open section in the brick wall below.
description_view_2: The rear gable end reveals the chimney on the left and the open wooden supports beneath the roof.
description_view_3: The back view highlights the hip roof and wooden posts, with the brick wall partially visible on the right.
attribute_entryway_design: Large open section in the brick wall, no visible door, porch, or decorative entry features
